question: How does Xinpinmu specifically solve Temu's tariff problem, and what does the full value chain look like?

findings.claim: Temu operates 13 self-owned warehouses globally: 10 EU, 2 US, 1 Mexico (as of Oct 2025)
findings.data: 13 self-owned warehouses: EU (Germany, France, Spain, Netherlands, Italy, Austria, UK - 10 total), US (Rowland Heights CA, Dallas TX - 2), Mexico border ('Jitu Warehouse' - 120,000 sqm - 1). 14th planned for Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Also operates 165,000 sqm logistics center in Gimpo, South Korea.

findings.claim: Temu targets 80% of European orders fulfilled via local warehouses by 2026
findings.data: Temu announced goal of 80% of all European orders shipped from local warehouses. Started using local warehouses in Europe in December 2024. UK target: 50% local fulfillment by end of 2025. EU warehouse locations: Rotterdam, Frankfurt (largest/most advanced), plus UK, Germany, Austria, Italy, Spain, France.

findings.claim: Temu US self-operated warehouses projected to handle only 15-20% of US volume in 2025, rising to 20-25% by 2026
findings.data: Self-operated warehouses in US handle 15-20% of total US goods volume (2025 projection), rising to 20-25% by 2026. Self-operated warehousing will NOT become the primary delivery method in the US market. Primary function: storing best-selling products, providing initial support for semi-managed merchants. This suggests Xinpinmu-branded products would be the primary use case for these warehouses.

findings.claim: Amazon operates 150+ US fulfillment centers and 600+ total US logistics sites - dwarfing Temu's 2 US warehouses
findings.data: Amazon: 150+ US fulfillment centers, 600+ total logistics sites, 1,200+ globally. 35 FCs in California alone, 28 in Texas, 17 in New Jersey. 92% of Prime members within one-day ground zone. $15B plan for 80 new sites in 2025. 750K robotics units. By comparison, Temu has 2 US warehouses. This scale gap means Xinpinmu can only serve a small, curated product selection in the US initially.

findings.claim: Temu partnerships with major logistics providers for European local fulfillment
findings.data: Temu has forged partnerships with DHL, FedEx, Royal Mail, and bpostgroup for local delivery. This suggests Temu does not need to build last-mile delivery from scratch - it can leverage existing infrastructure. The Xinpinmu model would ship bulk to Temu warehouses, then hand off to these local carriers for delivery.
